title: First tree-recurrence complex
summary: Records stars-and-bars dimensions, a proposed Euler characteristic, the first recurrence differential, and a placeholder package for a future generic exactness proof.

mathematicalContent.heading: Expected dimensions
mathematicalContent.body: The number of degree-s exponent vectors on 2r−1 vertices is the stars-and-bars number C(s+2r−2,2r−2). The proposed generic kernel dimension is C(k+r−2,r−2).

mathematicalContent.heading: Euler characteristic target
mathematicalContent.body: A proposed recurrence complex has alternating sum over q, with natural subtraction k−q as implemented by Lean.

mathematicalContent.heading: Implemented differential
mathematicalContent.body: Only the first differential is present. Its kernel is definitionally the rank recurrence from TreeRecurrence.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the stars-and-bars count of degree-s exponent assignments on the left-comb vertices.
Defines the conjectured generic dimension of the first-differential kernel.
Defines the alternating Euler characteristic proposed for the rank-general recurrence complex.
States the target equality between the proposed Euler characteristic and expected kernel dimension.

correspondence.leanType: abbrev firstDifferential {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Fin r → CombCoeffSpace r s
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Aliases the first complex differential to the previously defined path-recurrence map d₁.

correspondence.leanType: def InFirstKernel {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines membership in the first kernel by literal vanishing of d₁.

correspondence.leanType: theorem recurrence_iff_in_first_kernel {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : RankRecurrenceAtLevel n w A ↔ InFirstKernel n w A
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the rank recurrence predicate is equivalent to membership in the first kernel.
Creates a placeholder proposition containing only a proof of the proposed Euler-characteristic formula.

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: Only d₁ and definitional kernel equivalence are implemented. Higher Koszul-type differentials, d²=0, exactness, the Euler-characteristic identity, and the claimed generic dimension are not proved; GenericExactnessData merely stores the target equality as an assumption.
